required_ports.purpose: Dante audio transport (unicast and multicast)
Dante device discovery and control
PTP / IEEE 1588 clock sync (event)
PTP / IEEE 1588 clock sync (general)

dante-audio.vlan_recommendation: Dedicated VLAN — Dante traffic must not share VLAN with general IT
dante-audio.av_justification: Dante AoIP requires low-latency multicast-capable network with IGMP snooping enabled and no spanning tree interruptions. Verified ports per Audinate firewall documentation: UDP 4440 (audio), 8700 (discovery), 319/320 (PTP).
notes: IGMP snooping must be enabled on all switches carrying Dante traffic. PTP grandmaster must be reachable on the same VLAN. Dante unicast flows use port 4440; multicast flows use addresses in the 239.x.x.x range.

required_ports.purpose: HTTPS management and API
Telnet DSP control (Crestron, Q-SYS, AMX integration)
HTTP management (redirect to HTTPS)
AES67 audio transport (SMPTE ST 2110)
PTP / IEEE 1588 clock sync (event)
PTP / IEEE 1588 clock sync (general)

biamp-tesira.vlan_recommendation: AV Audio VLAN or dedicated DSP VLAN with IGMP snooping enabled
biamp-tesira.av_justification: Biamp Tesira requires AES67 multicast audio transport (UDP 5004) and PTP sync (UDP 319/320). Telnet port 23 is required for control system integration (Crestron, Q-SYS). HTTPS is required for Tesira Server-IO and Firmware Manager.
notes: AES67 requires IGMP snooping on all switches. Telnet (port 23) must be permitted from control processor IP only. Refer to Biamp support article for Tesira Server and EX expander port differences.

required_ports.purpose: Q-SYS control protocol (QRC) — required for all controller integration
HTTPS management and Q-SYS Designer communication
HTTP management (redirects to HTTPS)
AES67 audio transport (when AES67 licence is active)
PTP / IEEE 1588 clock sync (event) — required with AES67
PTP / IEEE 1588 clock sync (general) — required with AES67

qsys-core.vlan_recommendation: AV Control VLAN. If AES67 is used, place on AV Audio VLAN with IGMP snooping enabled.
qsys-core.av_justification: Q-SYS Core DSP requires TCP 1710 (QRC) for all control system integration (Crestron, AMX, Extron, third-party). Port 443 is used by Q-SYS Designer and Remote Management. AES67 ports (5004, 319, 320) only required when AES67 audio networking licence is active.
notes: QRC port 1710 must be open from all control processors, external control systems, and UCI panels. AES67 ports only apply when the AES67 Software Licence is installed on the Core.

required_ports.purpose: Crestron IP (CIP) — touchpanel and device registration
Crestron Secure CIP — encrypted device registration
Crestron Toolbox / programming direct connect
HTTPS management web UI and XiO Cloud
SSH management access
Telnet legacy management (disable if unused)

crestron-processor.vlan_recommendation: AV Control VLAN. Must have bidirectional TCP access to all controlled AV endpoints.
crestron-processor.av_justification: Crestron control processors require CIP ports 41794/41795 for touchpanel, UC device, and peripheral registration. Port 443 is required for web management and XiO Cloud connectivity. Port 41796 is used only during programming via Crestron Toolbox.
notes: CIP (41794) is unencrypted — prefer Secure CIP (41795) in security-conscious deployments. Disable Telnet (23) if not required. Processor must be able to initiate outbound TCP to all controlled devices.

required_ports.purpose: Secure CIP session to Crestron control processor
HTTPS to Crestron control processor / XiO Cloud
HTTP to control processor (legacy)

crestron-touchpanel.vlan_recommendation: AV Control VLAN. Must reach Crestron control processor on same or adjacent VLAN.
crestron-touchpanel.av_justification: Crestron touch panels connect outbound to the control processor via Secure CIP. Panels placed on user-facing VLANs require firewall rules permitting outbound TCP 41795 to the processor.
notes: Ensure touch panel IP can reach the control processor IP on TCP 41795 and 443. XiO Cloud-registered panels also require outbound internet access on 443.

required_ports.purpose: HTTPS management and XiO Cloud communication
Secure CIP — Crestron control processor connection
SIP signalling to PBX / call manager
RTP media transport for SIP audio/video calls

crestron-uc-engine.vlan_recommendation: AV Control or Corporate VLAN. Requires SIP trunk access if UC calling is enabled.
crestron-uc-engine.av_justification: UC engine handles unified communications for AV spaces. TCP 5060 is required when SIP calling is enabled; RTP (UDP 5004) carries the media streams. Secure CIP connects the UC engine to the room control processor.
notes: SIP and RTP ports only required when Microsoft Teams / Zoom / SIP calling is configured. Verify RTP port range with PBX/call manager as dynamic RTP ports may extend beyond 5004.

required_ports.purpose: HTTPS management and Q-SYS Designer communication
RTSP video stream session setup
Q-SYS Core control — outbound from endpoint to Core
AES67 / video-over-IP transport
PTP / IEEE 1588 clock sync (event)
PTP / IEEE 1588 clock sync (general)
HTTP management (redirects to HTTPS)

qsys-nv-endpoint.vlan_recommendation: Dedicated AV Video VLAN. Must have multicast routing or unicast reachability to Q-SYS Core.
qsys-nv-endpoint.av_justification: Q-SYS NV endpoints encode/decode AV-over-IP streams. TCP 1710 (outbound) connects the endpoint to the Q-SYS Core as a peripheral. RTSP (554) handles stream session setup. UDP 5004 carries the video/audio payload. PTP sync is required for frame-accurate switching.
notes: NV endpoints must be able to reach the Q-SYS Core on TCP 1710 outbound. Multicast IGMP is required for video distribution. PTP grandmaster must be accessible on the AV Video VLAN.

required_ports.purpose: HTTPS web management interface
HTTP web management (typically redirects to HTTPS)

generic-web-device.vlan_recommendation: Management VLAN or AV VLAN as appropriate to device function
generic-web-device.av_justification: Fallback profile for AV devices with web-based management interfaces not covered by a specific vendor profile. Assign specific vendor profile when available.
notes: This is a fallback baseline. Always prefer a specific vendor profile. Verify actual management ports in device documentation.

required_ports.purpose: HTTPS web management and ClickShare app connection
HTTP management (redirects to HTTPS)
mDNS — ClickShare device auto-discovery by presenter devices
Miracast RTSP session setup (optional wireless display feature)

barco-clickshare.vlan_recommendation: Wireless Presentation VLAN or Corporate VLAN. Presenter devices (laptops, phones) must reach ClickShare base unit. mDNS (UDP 5353) must traverse the network segment.
barco-clickshare.av_justification: Barco ClickShare uses HTTPS (443) for the ClickShare app and web management. mDNS (UDP 5353) is required for automatic base unit discovery by presenter devices on the same network segment. Miracast (TCP 7236) is optional and only required if the Miracast wireless display feature is used.
notes: mDNS (UDP 5353) does not cross router boundaries — ClickShare and presenter devices must be on the same Layer 2 segment, or an mDNS proxy must be configured. Disable Miracast port 7236 if the feature is not required. AirPlay requires additional ports — consult Barco documentation if AirPlay is needed.

required_ports.purpose: RTP / AES67 AV-over-IP transport (audio and video)
RTCP stream quality control and statistics
PTP / IEEE 1588 clock sync (event)
PTP / IEEE 1588 clock sync (general)
HTTPS management interface

av-over-ip.vlan_recommendation: Dedicated AV Video VLAN with IGMP snooping. Do not mix with general IT traffic.
av-over-ip.av_justification: AV-over-IP encoders and decoders require multicast UDP transport (RTP/AES67) with PTP clock synchronisation. IGMP snooping is mandatory to prevent multicast flooding across all switch ports. RTCP (5005) provides real-time stream quality monitoring.
notes: Ensure IGMP querier is active on the AV VLAN. PTP requires low-latency switch forwarding; use PTP-aware (boundary clock) switches where possible. Verify vendor documentation for specific VLAN tagging and QoS DSCP requirements.

required_ports.purpose: HTTPS management web UI
HTTP management web UI (redirect to HTTPS)

display.vlan_recommendation: AV VLAN. Management access permitted from AV Control VLAN only.
display.av_justification: Managed commercial displays require network access for remote power control, input switching, and monitoring via Crestron, Q-SYS, or manufacturer-specific control drivers. HTTPS is the standard management interface.
notes: Many commercial displays also support RS-232 control (not a network port). LAN control protocols vary by manufacturer (e.g., Samsung MDC, LG Sicp, Sony BRAVIA). Verify the specific display model's IP control port requirements — some use proprietary TCP ports in addition to HTTP/S.

required_ports.purpose: RTSP video stream session setup (ONVIF standard)
HTTPS management web UI
HTTP management / ONVIF device discovery
RTP video transport (multicast or unicast)

camera.vlan_recommendation: AV Camera VLAN or AV Video VLAN. Restrict inbound management access to AV Control VLAN only.
camera.av_justification: IP cameras stream video via RTSP (TCP 554) — the standard for ONVIF-compliant cameras. HTTPS provides secure management access. RTP (UDP 5004) carries the actual video payload after RTSP session negotiation. PTZ control typically travels over the same HTTP/S management session.
notes: ONVIF cameras use RTSP on port 554 for stream setup; the RTP data port is negotiated during the RTSP session and may differ from 5004. Verify PTZ camera vendor documentation for any additional proprietary control ports.

required_ports.purpose: HTTPS management web UI
HTTP management web UI (redirect to HTTPS)
Telnet control from control processor (common for matrix routing)

video-switcher.vlan_recommendation: AV Control VLAN or AV Video VLAN. Control processor must have TCP reachability to switcher.
video-switcher.av_justification: Video matrix switchers require HTTPS for web management and Telnet (TCP 23) for control system integration (Crestron, Q-SYS, AMX routing commands). Telnet is the most common control interface for Extron, Kramer, and similar matrix switchers.
notes: Telnet (port 23) is commonly used for matrix control integration — permit only from control processor IPs. Some switchers use vendor-specific TCP ports for control; verify manufacturer documentation. Physical matrix connections (HDBT, fibre) are outside the IP network scope.

required_ports.purpose: NDI discovery and control channel
NDI stream data channel (primary video/audio transport)
mDNS — NDI source auto-discovery (required without NDI Discovery Server)

ndi.vlan_recommendation: AV Video VLAN. NDI requires low-latency network. Do not bridge with corporate VLAN without QoS policy.
ndi.av_justification: NDI (Network Device Interface) transmits professional-grade video over standard IP using TCP 5960/5961. mDNS (UDP 5353) is required for automatic source discovery unless an NDI Discovery Server is deployed. NDI is a NewTek/Vizrt proprietary protocol.
notes: NDI is bandwidth-intensive: 1080p60 requires approximately 125 Mbps per stream. Deploy an NDI Discovery Server to replace mDNS in routed or large-scale environments — this eliminates the mDNS broadcast requirement. NDI streams use TCP so no IGMP snooping is required.

required_ports.purpose: RTMP live stream push to CDN / streaming platform
HTTPS management and RTMPS encrypted stream output
HTTP management interface

streaming-encoder.vlan_recommendation: AV VLAN with internet egress, or Corporate VLAN with access to streaming CDN endpoint.
streaming-encoder.av_justification: Streaming encoders push live content via RTMP (TCP 1935) to CDN platforms (YouTube, Vimeo, Restream) or RTMPS (encrypted RTMP over TLS, TCP 443). Outbound TCP 1935 must be permitted to the streaming destination IP.
notes: Most modern streaming platforms require RTMPS (RTMP over TLS on port 443) rather than plain RTMP on 1935. Confirm the customer's streaming platform requirements. Encoder management HTTPS (inbound 443) should be restricted to AV Control VLAN.

required_ports.purpose: RTP multicast IPTV transport
RTSP stream control and session setup
HTTPS management interface
HTTP management (redirect to HTTPS)

iptv.vlan_recommendation: Dedicated IPTV VLAN or AV Video VLAN with IGMP snooping enabled on all switches.
iptv.av_justification: IPTV systems rely on UDP multicast (RTP, UDP 5004) for efficient channel distribution. IGMP snooping prevents multicast flooding across all switch ports. RTSP (TCP 554) handles channel selection and stream session control.
notes: An IGMP querier must be active on the IPTV VLAN. The IPTV head-end server must be reachable from all decoder endpoints. Verify specific system ports — IPTV platform vendors (ZeeVee, Visionary Solutions, etc.) may use additional proprietary ports.

required_ports.purpose: HTTPS app connection and management interface
mDNS — device auto-discovery by presenter devices
Miracast RTSP session setup (optional wireless display standard)

wireless-presentation.vlan_recommendation: Wireless Presentation VLAN or bridged Corporate VLAN. Presenter devices must reach base unit on TCP 443 and UDP 5353.
wireless-presentation.av_justification: Wireless presentation base units require HTTPS (443) for secure app connection and web management. mDNS (UDP 5353) is used for automatic discovery of the base unit by presenter devices on the same network segment. Miracast (TCP 7236) adds Wi-Fi Direct screen mirroring for Windows devices.
notes: This is a generic profile. Specific systems (Mersive Solstice, Crestron AirMedia, Barco ClickShare, Extron ShareLink) each have their own additional port requirements — consult vendor documentation. mDNS does not cross router boundaries without an mDNS proxy. Disable Miracast (7236) if the feature is not used.

required_ports.purpose: HTTPS management and API
Telnet control from control system (common for third-party DSP integration)
SSH management access

audio-dsp.vlan_recommendation: AV Audio VLAN or AV Control VLAN. Must be reachable from control processors.
audio-dsp.av_justification: Generic audio DSP profile for DSP hardware not covered by a specific vendor profile (Biamp, Q-SYS). Control system integration commonly uses Telnet (TCP 23) for text-based command/response protocols.
notes: Always prefer a specific vendor profile (biamp-tesira, qsys-core) over this generic profile. Verify DSP vendor documentation for actual control ports. Restrict Telnet access to control processor IPs only.

required_ports.purpose: RTP media transport — voice and video
SIP signalling (unencrypted) — to PBX / call manager
SIP TLS signalling (encrypted) — preferred over port 5060
HTTPS management and cloud service registration (Cisco, Poly, Teams)
H.323 call signalling (legacy — disable if SIP only)

vc-codec.vlan_recommendation: AV Conferencing VLAN or Corporate VLAN with QoS DSCP marking. Must access SIP trunk, CUCM, or cloud service endpoint.
vc-codec.av_justification: Video conferencing codecs require SIP signalling (TCP 5060/5061) to a PBX or cloud service and RTP (UDP 5004) for media. HTTPS (443) is required for management and cloud platform registration (Cisco Webex, Microsoft Teams Rooms, Zoom Rooms). Prefer SIP TLS (5061) over plain SIP (5060).
notes: RTP port 5004 is the base — actual RTP media may use a dynamic port range (e.g., 16384–32767 for Cisco, 5004–6000 for Poly). Verify with the specific codec vendor and call manager. H.323 (1720) is legacy — disable if the deployment uses SIP only.

required_ports.purpose: SSH management — primary secure management access
HTTPS management web UI
SNMP polling from network management system (NMS)
SNMP trap — outbound from switch to NMS

network-switch.vlan_recommendation: Management VLAN. Switch management interfaces must not be on AV data VLANs.
network-switch.av_justification: Network switches require SSH and HTTPS for secure management and configuration (VLAN setup, IGMP snooping, QoS policy). SNMP (UDP 161/162) enables monitoring from NMS platforms. Management access should be restricted to the management VLAN.
notes: Switch management should be isolated on a dedicated management VLAN accessible only from authorised management hosts. Disable HTTP (80) and Telnet (23) management if SSH and HTTPS are available. SNMPv3 is strongly preferred over SNMPv1/v2c for security.

required_ports.purpose: HTTPS management and wireless controller communication (CAPWAP over TLS)
SSH management access
SNMP polling from NMS

wireless-ap.vlan_recommendation: Management VLAN for AP management interface. AP data plane uses trunk ports bridging AV and corporate VLANs.
wireless-ap.av_justification: Wireless APs in AV spaces serve presenter devices, conferencing systems, and UC peripherals. Management access on HTTPS and SSH must be isolated from client VLANs. Cloud-managed APs require outbound HTTPS to the cloud controller.
notes: Cloud-managed APs (Cisco Meraki, Aruba Central, Ubiquiti UniFi) require outbound HTTPS to vendor cloud controllers — verify specific FQDN and port requirements. CAPWAP (UDP 5246/5247) is required if using a wireless LAN controller. Confirm vendor documentation for controller-specific ports.
